input.background: The time to failure of a component follows a gamma distribution with parameters \( \alpha = 3 \) and \( \beta = 5 \). The problem involves determining the reliability of the component and the hazard rate at 10 time-units, as well as understanding the properties of the gamma distribution.
input.question: Determine the hazard rate of the component at 10 time-units.

output.answer: The hazard rate is given by $$ h(10)=\frac{f(10)}{R(10)}=\frac{0.054}{0.6767}=0.798 \ \text{failures/unit time} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
